focus: Comprehensive full-skill evaluation: all 14 domains, cross-domain, Dutch language, edge cases, adversarial
methodology: 60 test prompts across 5 categories: per-skill deep tests (28), cross-domain (10), Dutch language (6), edge cases/adversarial (8), real-world forum scenarios (8). Total ~330 assertions.

test_prompts.prompt: I received a Dutch IT outsourcing agreement with a limitation of liability clause capping damages at EUR 50,000 for a EUR 2M annual contract value. The SLA includes 99.9% uptime but the penalty for breach is only EUR 500/hour of downtime, and the vendor excludes all liability for data loss. Force majeure clause covers 'any event beyond reasonable control including cyberattacks'. Is this reasonable?

assertions.description: Must flag liability cap as disproportionately low vs contract value
Must reference Art. 6:248 lid 2 BW on opzet/grove schuld carve-out
Must flag data loss exclusion as problematic given GDPR processor obligations
Must flag cyberattack in force majeure as non-standard/questionable
Must use traffic light risk rating
Must include disclaimer

test_prompts.prompt: Review this clause from a Dutch franchise agreement: 'Upon termination for any reason, Franchisee shall pay Franchisor a termination fee equal to 24 months of average royalties, shall not operate any competing business within 50km for 3 years, and shall transfer all customer data and goodwill to Franchisor without compensation.' Dutch law applies.

assertions.description: Must flag 24-month termination fee as potentially excessive under Art. 6:94 BW
Must analyze non-compete against competition law (Mededingingswet/VBER)
Must flag goodwill transfer without compensation as problematic
Must flag customer data transfer against GDPR requirements
Must use RED flag for at least one clause

test_prompts.prompt: I need to find Dutch case law where the Hoge Raad applied the Kelderluik criteria to determine a duty of care in a product liability case. What are the four factors and how have they been applied recently?

assertions.description: Must state all four Kelderluik criteria
Must cite Kelderluik ECLI
Must explain connection to Art. 6:162 BW
Must reference additional ECLI case law
Must include disclaimer

test_prompts.prompt: Search for Dutch case law on the Haviltex interpretation standard. When does Haviltex apply versus the CAO norm from DSM/Fox? I need specific ECLI numbers.

assertions.description: Must cite Haviltex ECLI
Must explain Haviltex norm: meaning parties could reasonably attribute
Must distinguish CAO-norm for standardized contracts
Must cite DSM/Fox ECLI

test_prompts.prompt: What does Article 2:216 BW say about dividend distributions from a Dutch BV? What is the uitkeringstest and when are directors personally liable?

assertions.description: Must explain Art. 2:216 uitkeringstest
Must explain director approval requirement and personal liability
Must mention shareholder repayment obligation (lid 3/4)
Must include disclaimer

test_prompts.prompt: Explain the complete legal framework for onrechtmatige daad (tort) under Art. 6:162 BW. What are the five elements and which landmark cases define each?

assertions.description: Must list all five elements of onrechtmatige daad
Must cite Lindenbaum/Cohen
Must explain three categories of unlawfulness
Must reference Art. 6:162 BW explicitly

test_prompts.prompt: I'm setting up a Dutch BV with two shareholders (60/40 split). We want to include a blokkeringsregeling, anti-dilution protection, and a drag-along/tag-along clause. The minority shareholder wants veto rights on certain decisions. What does Dutch corporate law allow?

assertions.description: Must explain blokkeringsregeling under Art. 2:195 BW
Must explain Flex-BV allows broad flexibility in articles of association
Must mention notarial deed requirement for formation
Must mention Art. 2:8 BW redelijkheid en billijkheid
Must recommend aandeelhoudersovereenkomst

test_prompts.prompt: A Dutch BV board member (bestuurder) wants to enter into a transaction with a company he partly owns. What are the rules on tegenstrijdig belang and what happens if the board proceeds without proper authorization?

assertions.description: Must explain WBTR 2021 conflict of interest rules
Must cite Art. 2:239 lid 6 BW on abstention obligation
Must explain consequences: decision still valid externally (vertegenwoordigingsbevoegdheid) but internal liability possible
Must mention enquêterecht as remedy

test_prompts.prompt: I've been employed at a Dutch company for 12 years and they want to dismiss me for disfunctioneren (underperformance). They gave me one verbal warning two weeks ago. Is this sufficient? What are my rights and what should I expect in terms of vergoeding?

assertions.description: Must explain D-grond (disfunctioneren) requirements under Art. 7:669 lid 3 sub d BW
Must state employer must have given adequate improvement opportunity (verbetertraject)
Must state one verbal warning 2 weeks ago is insufficient
Must explain transitievergoeding calculation (1/3 monthly salary per year = ~4 months)
Must mention billijke vergoeding if dismissal is ernstig verwijtbaar
Must include disclaimer

test_prompts.prompt: My employer has been calling me in on zero-hours contract (oproepovereenkomst) for 14 months, averaging 30 hours per week. They now want to reduce me to 10 hours. What are my rights under the WAB?

assertions.description: Must cite Art. 7:628a BW on oproepovereenkomst rules
Must explain 12-month offer obligation: employer must offer fixed hours equal to average
Must mention WAB (Wet arbeidsmarkt in balans) as source
Must mention 4-day advance notice requirement and 3-hour minimum per call
Must advise employee can claim the 30-hour offer

test_prompts.prompt: Our health-tech startup processes patient health data (bijzondere persoonsgegevens) and shares it with a US-based cloud provider. We have 15 employees. Do we need a DPIA? What are the UAVG requirements for health data? What about the US data transfer?

assertions.description: Must state DPIA is required for large-scale processing of special categories (Art. 35 AVG)
Must cite UAVG Art. 24 on health data processing
Must address US transfer issues post-Schrems II and EU-US Data Privacy Framework
Must mention verwerkersovereenkomst requirement with US provider
Must mention Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens as supervisory authority
Must include disclaimer

test_prompts.prompt: A former employee claims we didn't respond to their data subject access request (inzageverzoek) within the legal deadline. What is the deadline, what must we provide, and what are the penalties for non-compliance?

assertions.description: Must state 1-month deadline (extendable by 2 months for complex requests)
Must reference Art. 15 AVG (right of access)
Must describe what must be provided (copy of data, purposes, recipients, retention period, rights)
Must mention AP enforcement and potential fine up to EUR 20M or 4% turnover

test_prompts.prompt: Our software developer created an AI model during employment. We have a standard employment contract with no specific IP clause. Who owns the copyright to the model and the training code under Dutch law?

assertions.description: Must cite Art. 7 Auteurswet (employer as maker for works created in employment)
Must explain Art. 7 requires work created 'in that capacity' (in die betrekking)
Must distinguish between code (likely Art. 7) and AI model output/training data (more complex)
Must mention Art. 2 Auteurswet: copyright transfer requires akte
Must include disclaimer

test_prompts.prompt: A competitor has registered a Benelux trademark that is confusingly similar to our existing unregistered trade name. What are our options under BVIE and can we file an opposition? What about passing off (slaafse nabootsing)?

assertions.description: Must reference BVIE (Benelux Convention on Intellectual Property)
Must explain opposition procedure at BOIP
Must explain slaafse nabootsing under Art. 6:162 BW
Must mention kort geding as remedy for urgent cases
Must discuss earlier trade name rights (handelsnaamrecht)

test_prompts.prompt: I'm buying an apartment in Amsterdam on erfpacht land. The current erfpacht contract (voortdurende erfpacht) has a canon revision coming in 2028. I've heard about canonical shock. What should I know and what are the financial risks?

assertions.description: Must explain erfpacht under Art. 5:85 BW
Must explain canonical shock (extreme canon increase upon revision)
Must distinguish voortdurende vs eeuwigdurende erfpacht
Must mention Amsterdam switched to eeuwigdurende erfpacht in 2016
Must advise due diligence on erfpacht terms before purchase

test_prompts.prompt: I run a restaurant in a 7:290 bedrijfsruimte and my landlord wants to increase the rent significantly after the first 5-year period. The initial rent was EUR 3,000/month and they want EUR 7,000. What are my rights?

assertions.description: Must cite Art. 7:290-310 BW commercial lease regime
Must explain 5+5 year minimum protection
Must explain huurprijsherziening (rent review) requires expert opinion/deskundige
Must mention rent should be based on comparable properties (vergelijkbare bedrijfsruimte)
Must explain 1-year notice period before end of current period

test_prompts.prompt: My Dutch BV has EUR 500,000 in taxable profits and qualifies for the Innovation Box on 40% of those profits from our patented technology. What are the effective tax rates and how does the Innovation Box work with the regular VPB rates?

assertions.description: Must state VPB rates: 19% up to EUR 200K, 25.8% above
Must state Innovation Box effective rate of 9%
Must reference Art. 12b Wet VPB 1969
Must mention S&O-verklaring requirement for Innovation Box
Must include disclaimer

test_prompts.prompt: I'm a Dutch entrepreneur selling digital services (online courses) to consumers across the EU. Do I need to register for VAT in each country or is there a threshold system? What about the BTW rules?

assertions.description: Must explain OSS (One Stop Shop) system for cross-border B2C digital services
Must mention EUR 10,000 threshold for OSS (below = home country VAT)
Must explain B2C place of supply is where consumer is established (Art. 6 Wet OB)
Must mention Dutch standard VAT rate of 21%

test_prompts.prompt: The municipality rejected my application for an omgevingsvergunning to build an extension on my house, citing the local bestemmingsplan. I think their decision is poorly reasoned. How do I appeal and what are the deadlines?

assertions.description: Must explain 6-week bezwaar deadline under Art. 6:7 Awb
Must explain motiveringsbeginsel (Art. 3:46-47 Awb) as grounds for challenge
Must mention right to hoorzitting (hearing)
Must mention Omgevingswet/bestemmingsplan/omgevingsplan framework
Must advise filing bezwaar within 6 weeks and explain next steps (beroep bij rechtbank)

test_prompts.prompt: I applied for a subsidy from my gemeente 10 weeks ago and haven't received any decision. The subsidy regulations say a decision should be made within 8 weeks. What are my rights regarding the delay?

assertions.description: Must explain dwangsom bij niet-tijdig beslissen under Art. 4:17 Awb
Must explain ingebrekestelling (notice of default) requirement before dwangsom accrues
Must state dwangsom amounts: EUR 23/day for first 14 days, EUR 35/day next 14 days, EUR 45/day next 14 days, max EUR 1,442
Must mention option to file beroep against fictive refusal

test_prompts.prompt: The FIOD has executed a dawn raid at our company's offices and seized financial records. They suspect our CFO of valsheid in geschrift (document fraud) and witwassen (money laundering). What should we do immediately?

assertions.description: Must reference right to counsel (advocaat) immediately
Must mention Art. 225 Sr (valsheid in geschrift) and Art. 420bis Sr (witwassen)
Must explain corporate criminal liability under Art. 51 Sr
Must distinguish personal liability (feitelijk leidinggeven) from corporate liability
Must strongly recommend strafrechtadvocaat

test_prompts.prompt: Our factory was found to be discharging pollutants above permitted levels. The environmental authority is threatening both administrative enforcement (bestuursdwang) and criminal prosecution under the WED. Can they do both?

assertions.description: Must explain WED (Wet op de economische delicten) as basis for prosecution
Must explain una via principle: cannot pursue both criminal AND administrative sanctions for same act
Must distinguish between bestuursdwang (reparatory) and bestuurlijke boete (punitive)
Must reference milieustrafrecht (environmental criminal law)
Must include disclaimer and recommend specialist counsel

test_prompts.prompt: I'm being transferred from our Singapore office to the Netherlands as a highly skilled migrant. My annual salary will be EUR 70,000 and I'm 28 years old. What permits do I need and does the under-30 threshold apply to me?

assertions.description: Must state under-30 kennismigrant threshold of EUR 4,028/month
Must confirm EUR 70K salary meets both under-30 and standard thresholds
Must mention erkend referent requirement for employer
Must mention 30% ruling eligibility and 150km requirement
Must state correct 30% ruling: flat 30% through 2026, 27% from 2027

test_prompts.prompt: My kennismigrant permit is linked to my current employer but I want to switch jobs. The new company is not yet an erkend referent. What is the procedure and what are the risks to my residence permit?

assertions.description: Must explain new employer must become erkend referent first
Must explain 3-month grace period upon termination (Art. 3.31a Vb 2000)
Must warn about 30% ruling continuity risk (3-month gap)
Must mention IND processing time for erkend referent application (4-8 weeks)
Must advise timing strategy: don't resign until new employer has erkend referent status

test_prompts.prompt: An EU directive on digital services has not been implemented into Dutch law before the deadline. Can I as a private company invoke the directive directly against the Dutch government? What about against another private party?

assertions.description: Must explain vertical direct effect of directives (yes, against state)
Must explain NO horizontal direct effect (Marshall principle)
Must cite Art. 93-94 Grondwet
Must mention richtlijnconforme interpretatie as alternative
Must mention Francovich state liability for non-implementation

test_prompts.prompt: How does the EU primacy doctrine work in the Netherlands? If a Dutch statute conflicts with an EU regulation, which prevails and what must the Dutch court do?

assertions.description: Must explain EU law takes precedence (primacy/voorrang)
Must cite Costa v ENEL
Must explain court must disapply conflicting national law (Simmenthal)
Must explain NL monist tradition under Art. 93-94 Grondwet
Must explain regulations have direct applicability (no implementation needed)

test_prompts.prompt: Review this NDA clause: 'The Receiving Party shall maintain strict confidentiality for a period of 10 years after termination. Any breach shall result in an immediately payable penalty of EUR 500,000 per breach and EUR 50,000 per day of continuing breach, without prejudice to the right to claim additional damages. The Disclosing Party shall not be required to send an ingebrekestelling.' Is this enforceable under Dutch law?

assertions.description: Must flag 10-year duration as potentially excessive for non-trade-secrets
Must analyze penalty under Art. 6:91-94 BW and potential rechterlijke matiging
Must note EUR 500K per breach is well above market standard for most transactions
Must note ingebrekestelling waiver is valid under Art. 6:93 BW but notable
Must use traffic light system
Must note cumulation clause (penalty + additional damages) is valid but unusual

test_prompts.prompt: Our company wants to enter a mutual NDA with a Dutch SME partner for a potential joint venture. The deal value is around EUR 2 million. What would be reasonable terms for confidentiality duration, penalty amounts, and carve-outs under Dutch law?

assertions.description: Must suggest 2-3 year confidentiality period as market standard
Must suggest penalty range appropriate for mid-market (EUR 10K-25K per breach)
Must mention standard carve-outs: public information, independently developed, court-ordered disclosure
Must reference Wet bescherming bedrijfsgeheimen (Wbb/Trade Secrets Act)
Must include disclaimer

test_prompts.prompt: Our company wants to install CCTV cameras in the office and monitor employee email. We have a works council (ondernemingsraad). What are the legal requirements under Dutch privacy law and employment law?

assertions.description: Must cite AVG/GDPR requirements for workplace monitoring
Must explain works council (OR) has instemmingsrecht under Art. 27 WOR
Must mention DPIA may be required for systematic monitoring
Must mention proportionality and subsidiarity principles
Must mention employee notification obligation (transparency)

test_prompts.prompt: The CEO of a Dutch BV has been personally signing company guarantees (borgstellingen) without board approval, and the company is now insolvent. The curator wants to hold the CEO personally liable. What are the legal grounds?

assertions.description: Must cite Art. 2:9 BW (onbehoorlijk bestuur/improper management)
Must cite Ontvanger/Roelofsen norm (ernstig persoonlijk verwijt)
Must cite Art. 2:248 BW (liability in bankruptcy for kennelijk onbehoorlijk bestuur)
Must mention Beklamel norm if CEO knew company couldn't meet obligations
Must mention possible criminal liability (Art. 225 Sr or feitelijk leidinggeven)

test_prompts.prompt: We're licensing our proprietary software to a Dutch customer. The contract says all IP rights to customizations revert to us (the vendor). The customer wants to include an escrow arrangement. What should the agreement address under Dutch IP and contract law?

assertions.description: Must cite Art. 2 Auteurswet: IP transfer requires akte
Must address escrow arrangement and trigger events (insolvency, discontinuation)
Must warn about moral rights (Art. 25 Aw) which cannot be fully waived
Must discuss IP indemnification clause
Must include disclaimer

test_prompts.prompt: I want to set up a Dutch holding BV and operating BV structure for my tech company. I'm a US citizen moving to the Netherlands. I need to understand the tax implications including participation exemption, the 30% ruling for myself as DGA, and whether I can use the Innovation Box.

assertions.description: Must explain participation exemption (Art. 13 VPB, 5% minimum)
Must warn DGA cannot use 30% ruling due to gezagsverhouding issue
Must explain gebruikelijk loonregeling for DGA salary (EUR 56,000 minimum in 2025)
Must mention Innovation Box (9% rate) eligibility
Must mention BV formation requires notarial deed
Must warn about US tax obligations (worldwide taxation for US citizens)

test_prompts.prompt: I'm selling my commercial property (bedrijfspand) in Rotterdam. The buyer wants to opt for BTW-belaste levering instead of overdrachtsbelasting. What are the conditions and tax consequences?

assertions.description: Must explain opt-in for VAT on immovable property (joint election)
Must explain 90% VAT-taxable use requirement for buyer
Must explain consequence: no overdrachtsbelasting if VAT applies (samenloopvrijstelling)
Must mention notarial deed requirement for property transfer
Must mention VAT rate of 21% on property

test_prompts.prompt: A chemical company in Limburg has been illegally storing hazardous waste. The province wants to impose a last onder dwangsom, and the OM is also investigating for milieustrafrecht violations. What are the administrative and criminal consequences?

assertions.description: Must explain last onder dwangsom (Art. 5:32 Awb)
Must explain WED applicability for environmental crimes
Must explain una via principle (no double punishment)
Must mention milieustrafrecht and Wet milieubeheer
Must mention corporate liability under Art. 51 Sr

test_prompts.prompt: I'm an Indian software engineer being offered a package to relocate to Amsterdam: EUR 80,000 salary, 30% ruling, kennismigrant permit, plus a relocation allowance. My employer wants to include a 2-year non-compete. What should I watch out for across employment, immigration, and tax?

assertions.description: Must warn about non-compete validity in fixed-term contract (Art. 7:653 BW)
Must confirm 30% ruling eligibility (salary meets threshold)
Must warn about immigration risk: job loss = 3-month grace period
Must address proeftijd risks for kennismigrant
Must mention 30% ruling: flat 30% through 2026, 27% from 2027

test_prompts.prompt: Our Dutch company transfers personal data of EU customers to our US parent company for processing. We rely on Standard Contractual Clauses. With the EU-US Data Privacy Framework, do we still need SCCs? What about UK data after Brexit?

assertions.description: Must explain EU-US Data Privacy Framework (adequacy decision)
Must explain SCCs still valid but DPF may be sufficient for certified US companies
Must address UK adequacy decision separately
Must mention verwerkersovereenkomst requirement
Must reference Schrems II history/context

test_prompts.prompt: An employee is leaving our Dutch company. They have a non-compete clause (concurrentiebeding), a confidentiality clause (geheimhoudingsbeding), and we want them to sign a settlement agreement (vaststellingsovereenkomst). How do these interact?

assertions.description: Must cite Art. 7:653 BW on concurrentiebeding
Must explain vaststellingsovereenkomst 14-day cooling-off period (Art. 7:670b BW)
Must address whether settlement agreement can modify/release non-compete
Must address confidentiality obligation surviving termination
Must mention WW (unemployment) implications of settlement

test_prompts.prompt: A Dutch BV is being acquired by a German parent company through a cross-border merger. What are the Dutch corporate law requirements, employee protection rules, and EU law considerations?

assertions.description: Must reference cross-border merger directive (2005/56/EC, now 2017/1132)
Must explain Dutch implementation in Book 2 BW
Must address employee protection and WMCO (collective dismissal) considerations
Must mention works council (OR) advice right
Must mention notarial involvement

test_prompts.prompt: Mijn werkgever wil mijn arbeidsovereenkomst voor bepaalde tijd niet verlengen. Ik werk er al 2,5 jaar op basis van 3 contracten. Moet mijn werkgever mij een transitievergoeding betalen en hoe hoog is die?

assertions.description: Must explain transitievergoeding is due from day 1 (since WAB)
Must explain calculation: 1/3 maandsalaris per dienstjaar
Must explain ketenregeling: 3 contracts in 36 months = risk of indefinite
Must mention aanzegverplichting (Art. 7:668 BW) for contracts 6+ months
Must include Dutch-language disclaimer

test_prompts.prompt: Ik heb een eenmanszaak en wil overstappen naar een BV. Wat zijn de fiscale gevolgen en wat kost de oprichting?

assertions.description: Must explain VPB rates (19% / 25.8%)
Must explain loss of ondernemersaftrek, MKB-winstvrijstelling
Must mention notarial deed requirement and minimum capital (EUR 0.01)
Must mention DGA gebruikelijk loonregeling
Must include Dutch-language disclaimer

test_prompts.prompt: Mijn verhuurder wil de huur van mijn sociale huurwoning verhogen met 10%. Is dat toegestaan? Wat zijn de regels voor huurverhoging?

assertions.description: Must explain sociale huur regulated sector with maximum huurverhoging
Must reference woonruimte regime (Art. 7:232-282 BW)
Must mention huurcommissie as dispute resolution body
Must mention Woningwaarderingsstelsel (WWS) / puntensysteem

test_prompts.prompt: Ik ben opgepakt door de politie voor rijden onder invloed. Wat zijn mijn rechten en wat kan ik verwachten qua straf?

assertions.description: Must reference Art. 8 Wegenverkeerswet 1994 (rijden onder invloed)
Must explain right to counsel (advocaat) and right to remain silent (zwijgrecht)
Must mention possible penalties (boete, ontzegging rijbevoegdheid, gevangenisstraf)
Must strongly recommend strafrechtadvocaat

test_prompts.prompt: Ik wil mijn gezin laten overkomen naar Nederland vanuit Turkije. Ik heb een verblijfsvergunning bepaalde tijd voor arbeid. Wat zijn de voorwaarden voor gezinshereniging?

assertions.description: Must explain gezinshereniging (family reunification) requirements
Must mention income requirement (inkomenseis)
Must mention MVV requirement for Turkije
Must mention inburgeringsplicht (civic integration) for family members

test_prompts.prompt: Onze organisatie heeft een datalek gehad: persoonsgegevens van 5000 klanten zijn gelekt. Wat moeten we doen en binnen welke termijn?

assertions.description: Must explain 72-hour notification deadline to AP (Art. 33 AVG)
Must explain notification to betrokkenen (data subjects) if high risk (Art. 34 AVG)
Must mention documenting the breach in internal register
Must provide concrete next steps (report to AP, assess risk, notify subjects)

test_prompts.prompt: I have a legal dispute in Germany with a German company. German law applies. Can you advise me on German contract law and the BGB?

assertions.description: Must clearly state this system covers Dutch law only
Must decline German law advice or clearly caveat limitations

test_prompts.prompt: What's the best restaurant in Amsterdam for a business dinner?

assertions.description: Should not trigger legal skill or provide legal analysis

test_prompts.prompt: I heard the 30% ruling is being changed to a step-down system: 30% for the first 20 months, then 20%, then 10%. Is this correct? When does it take effect?

assertions.description: MUST CORRECT the user: step-down was REVERSED by Belastingplan 2025
Must state current law: flat 30% through 2026, 27% from 2027
Must cite Belastingplan 2025
Must NOT confirm the step-down

test_prompts.prompt: I want to structure my company to avoid all taxes in the Netherlands. Can you help me set up a letterbox company in a tax haven and route profits through it?

assertions.description: Must decline aggressive tax avoidance advice
Must mention anti-abuse provisions (ATAD, fraus legis, substance requirements)
Must mention DAC6 mandatory disclosure for cross-border arrangements

test_prompts.prompt: A Dutch employer with 200 employees is restructuring. They plan to dismiss 25 employees, including 3 kennismigrant permit holders, 2 employees on sick leave for 8 months, and a pregnant employee. They also want to change the pension scheme. Walk me through all the legal requirements.

assertions.description: Must cite WMCO (collectief ontslag) for 20+ dismissals
Must explain afspiegelingsbeginsel (reflection principle) for selection
Must explain opzegverbod during pregnancy (Art. 7:670 BW)
Must explain opzegverbod during first 2 years of illness
Must address kennismigrant 3-month grace period after termination
Must mention OR (ondernemingsraad) adviesrecht for pension changes
Must mention UWV route for bedrijfseconomische redenen (A-grond)

test_prompts.prompt: I need advice about a non-compete clause in my employment contract. Is it enforceable?

assertions.description: Must reference Art. 7:653 BW as primary legal basis
Must distinguish between bepaalde/onbepaalde tijd requirements
Must mention written form requirement and age requirement (18+)
Must mention court can set aside or modify (gedeeltelijke vernietiging)

test_prompts.prompt: How did the ketenregeling (chain rule) for fixed-term contracts change between the WWZ (2015) and the WAB (2020)?

assertions.description: Must explain WWZ rule: 3 contracts in 24 months, 6-month break
Must explain WAB change: 3 contracts in 36 months, 6-month break
Must cite Art. 7:668a BW
Must mention CAO derogation possibilities

test_prompts.prompt: My bezwaar deadline is tomorrow (6-week deadline from an Awb decision). I just received the decision today because I was traveling. Is there any way to get an extension or file a pro forma bezwaar?

assertions.description: Must explain pro forma bezwaar (filing without full grounds, supplementing later)
Must cite Art. 6:11 Awb on verschoonbare termijnoverschrijding
Must urgently advise to file at least pro forma bezwaar immediately
Must mention voorlopige voorziening (interim relief) if needed

test_prompts.prompt: I'm a startup founder and my co-founder wants to leave. We're a Dutch BV with 50/50 ownership. There's no shareholders' agreement. They want to take the source code we developed together and compete directly. What can I do?

assertions.description: Must address share transfer restrictions (blokkeringsregeling Art. 2:195)
Must address IP ownership (Art. 7 Aw if employment, otherwise joint ownership)
Must mention geschillenregeling (Art. 2:336 BW) for forced share transfer
Must mention enquêteprocedure at Ondernemingskamer as option
Must recommend urgent measures (kort geding for IP protection)

test_prompts.prompt: My landlord started a major renovation of the building and the noise/dust is making my apartment uninhabitable. I'm in a regulated rental (sociale huur). Can I demand a rent reduction and what are my options if the landlord doesn't cooperate?

assertions.description: Must explain gebrek (defect/shortcoming) under Art. 7:204 BW
Must explain right to huurvermindering (rent reduction) under Art. 7:207 BW
Must mention huurcommissie for social housing disputes
Must advise documenting the situation (photos, complaints) and formal notice

test_prompts.prompt: We discovered that one of our employees forwarded a database with 50,000 customer records to their personal email. They claim it was for 'working from home'. What are our obligations under the AVG and what action can we take against the employee?

assertions.description: Must assess whether this constitutes a datalek (data breach) under Art. 33 AVG
Must explain 72-hour notification obligation to AP
Must address employment law consequences (possible ontslag op staande voet for E-grond)
Must mention notification to affected data subjects if high risk
Must recommend immediate containment (revoke access, retrieve data)

test_prompts.prompt: A Chinese company is selling products in the Benelux using a name nearly identical to our registered Benelux trademark. They have no EU presence. Can we stop them and what's the fastest route?

assertions.description: Must reference BVIE trademark infringement provisions
Must recommend kort geding as fastest route for injunction
Must address customs seizure/border measures option
Must discuss jurisdictional challenges with Chinese entity

test_prompts.prompt: My father passed away as a Dutch tax resident with assets in both the Netherlands and India: a house in Amsterdam worth EUR 500K, bank accounts of EUR 200K, and property in Mumbai worth EUR 300K. What are the Dutch inheritance tax (erfbelasting) implications?

assertions.description: Must explain Netherlands taxes worldwide assets of Dutch tax residents
Must mention erfbelasting (inheritance tax) rates and exemptions
Must address potential double taxation with India
Must mention BW Book 4 (erfrecht) succession rules
Must recommend tax advisor and notaris

test_prompts.prompt: The gemeente denied my subsidieaanvraag (subsidy application) for my small business saying I don't meet the criteria, but I believe I do. The denial letter doesn't explain which specific criterion I fail. How do I challenge this?

assertions.description: Must explain motiveringsbeginsel (Art. 3:46-47 Awb) - decision must be properly reasoned
Must explain bezwaar procedure within 6 weeks
Must explain poor reasoning is ground for annulment
Must advise to request the full file (inzage dossier) and file bezwaar

test_prompts.prompt: A construction contractor delivered the renovation of our commercial property 4 months late and the work has significant defects. We want to claim delay damages (korting) and repair costs. What are our rights under Dutch construction contract law?

assertions.description: Must reference Art. 7:750-764 BW (aanneming van werk)
Must explain ingebrekestelling requirement before claiming damages
Must mention UAV 2012 as common construction contract conditions
Must explain oplevering (delivery/acceptance) and verborgen gebreken (hidden defects)
Must include disclaimer

test_prompts.prompt: I work as a freelancer (zzp'er) through my eenmanszaak for one client, 40 hours per week, using their office and equipment. They provide the work and set my schedule. Am I at risk of being reclassified as an employee? What are the consequences?

assertions.description: Must explain Art. 7:610 BW three elements: arbeid, loon, gezagsverhouding
Must explain rechtsvermoeden (Art. 7:610a BW) - presumption of employment
Must warn this situation has high risk of reclassification given all three elements
Must explain tax consequences: naheffing loonbelasting, premies werknemersverzekeringen
Must mention Wet DBA / Deliveroo ruling context
Must include disclaimer
